notes: 4 clues per country, ordered hardest/most obscure -> most obvious, matching the format of all previous batches. This completes the full 152-entry set alongside Americas, Europe, Africa, and Asia.

clues: Its capital was purpose-built as a compromise between two rival cities, Sydney and Melbourne.
It's the only country that is also an entire continent.
It's home to more venomous snake species than any other country.
It's famous for kangaroos, koalas, and the Sydney Opera House.
It's home to more sheep than people, though the ratio has shrunk over the decades.
It was the first country in the world to grant women the right to vote, in 1893.
Its capital is widely considered the windiest city in the world.
It's made up of two main islands in the South Pacific, southeast of Australia.
It's one of the most linguistically diverse countries in the world, with over 800 languages spoken.
It shares its island with Indonesia, split roughly down the middle.
Much of its interior remains covered by dense, largely unexplored rainforest.
It sits just north of Australia in the South Pacific.
It's made up of more than 300 islands, though only about a third are inhabited.
It sits directly on the 180th meridian, near the International Date Line.
It's one of the most visited island nations in the South Pacific for tourism.
It's an island country east of Australia.
It was the site of some of the fiercest Pacific battles of World War II, including Guadalcanal.
It's made up of nearly 1,000 islands.
Its capital is named after a nearby mountain range and river.
It sits in the South Pacific, northeast of Australia.
